Convert this single it.todo to an executable test. The test should verify runScenario produces a results array of the same length as the input steps array, even with multiple steps. Use a scenario with two quote steps (since claim handling isn't yet implemented in runScenario):
```
{customer:{yearsWithMHPCO:0}, steps:[
  {op:"quote", items:[{type:"sword",material:"steel",enchantment:3,cursed:false}]},
  {op:"quote", items:[{type:"amulet",material:"gold",enchantment:0,cursed:false}]}
]}
```
Expected: results array has length 2. (You can either assert just the length, or both lengths and entries.)

NOTE: After refactor, runScenario uses `filter(isQuoteStep).map(...)`. With two quote steps and the current implementation, this test should ALREADY PASS (no Red expected). However, the test is required to be activated. If it passes immediately on activation, document that and proceed.

Actually wait - re-check the implementation. With the previous refactor, runScenario filters by isQuoteStep and maps. Two quote steps would both be processed. So this test will likely pass immediately. If so, just activate it (no behavioral change needed).

You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or Incorrect).

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
